title: The Raw Appeal: Deconstructing the Aesthetics of Brutalist Architecture

questions.sentence: Brutalism an architectural style prevalent from the 1950s to the mid1970s has long been a subject of intense debate and polarized opinions
Characterized by monolithic forms exposed structural elements and a stark honesty of materials this style sought to make powerful often monumental statements about modern life and society
At its core Brutalism champions functionality and direct expression of structure
Raw concrete was not just cost effective but an aesthetic choice celebrating its inherent texture color and strength
The immense scale aimed to project civic power and permanence especially for public institutions and social housing
